reviews.review_text: Frustratingly challenging, but SUCH a fantastic game. Beating bosses actually feels rewarding and the game's style is so refreshing. Cuphead is PEAK and never gets old, I still play it after beating it years ago. Top tier.

Cuphead is honestly one of the best platformers I’ve ever played. The art style is absolutely amazing. 

The gameplay is challenging, but in a fun and rewarding way. Every boss feels unique, and beating a difficult level gives a great sense of satisfaction.

I didn’t run into any bugs or technical issues at all, and the game runs super smooth from start to finish. Everything feels responsive and well optimized.

Overall, Cuphead is an incredible game with great gameplay and good artstyle. Definitely a must-play if you enjoy platformers or challenging games

10/10 in its category
Easily a nice and enjoyable game, some Bosses are tedious but is part of the fun, great music and animation made with the heart. Recomended also to play with friends to lose your mind together.

I was surprisingly not frustrated at any bosses in the game. I've dealt with stressful games or achieving stressful objectives but this was just pleasantly okay. Yes I died at times yes I lost a few minutes to stupid mistakes but I had a smile on my face the entire time playing it because it was just so fun to play!

Also runs perfectly on silent mode (13w) on the ROG Ally X which gave me many hours to play

Cuphead is visually breathtaking. The hand-drawn 1930s cartoon animation is one of the most impressive art styles I’ve ever seen in a video game, and the music is so good. But beauty alone doesn’t make a game enjoyable, and after my time with it, I can’t recommend it for everyone.

This game is brutally hard, every boss fight demands perfect precision, pattern memorization, and lightning-fast reflexes. If you’re a newer player the learning curve will feel less like a curve and more like a brick wall. Even my veteran gamer friend has spent hours stuck on a single boss. If you’re buying this for a younger player or someone just getting into gaming, I’d strongly caution against it. It’s punishing in a way that stops being fun pretty quickly.

I had high hopes for co-op through Steam’s Remote Play Together, but honestly it was nearly unplayable for me. The input lag was severe, and in a game where surviving depends entirely on split-second timing to dodge attacks, any lag at all makes things nearly impossible. 

Bottom Line
If you’re a gamer who lives for a hardcore challenge and you’re playing solo on your own machine, Cuphead might be exactly what you’re looking for. But if you’re newer to gaming, buying it for a younger player, or hoping to enjoy it with a friend over Remote Play Together, I’d say either go in with very low expectations or skip it altogether.

Easily one of the greatest platformers I've ever played, it's basically perfect. Control response is sharp (I used a PS5 controller, the D pad gives the precision I feel I really needed) and if you make a mistake it's 100% on you and not the controls, I love games like that. 

It's also worth mentioning that Kristofer Maddigan was my most listened to artist of the year I played this, because the soundtrack is PHENOMENAL I was obsessed!! The jazz vibes are unlike anything I've heard in a game, also I don't think many games can say they have actual tap dancing in its music 😍

Cuphead is also one of the few games I've truly understood the appeal of gruelling difficulty and feeling actual rage I felt like crying, because the ultimate high of finally beating the biggest challenges in the game also lowkey made me cry 😂 Absolutely unbelievably well balanced challenge in my opinion, I had to come back and beat the whole thing on expert and got all the achievements, just loved the suffering that much. The DLC also just adds more of the same great content, awesome art style and love-hate relationship pain. Mostly love though.

Liked the main game pretty much, it was frustrating at times but mostly fun. DLC wasn't as fun, attack patterns became more unpredictable, sometimes I just couldn't understand what's going on because of hundreds of projectiles flying around my screen, but it still was pretty good, so overall I can recommend at least the main game. DLC is not bad, but I don't know if I would recommend it.
I got really mad from this game and I couldn't punch my monitor because it's too expensive so I punched myself in the face instead. Now I have a permanent scar on my nose to remember this game.

I really wanted to like this game i really did, but it has some big issues

1. Difficulty curve is to steep & bs
The first 3 bosses are dirt easy but once you get to the flower it gets a lot harder from there. The run and guns arent much better. Some bosses are so hard

2. No level climax?
I swear to god, defeating bosses doesnt feel like a challenge, you just hold *insert attack* button and dodge attacks, often being dirt easy to dodge, but the unforgiving health pool makes it a pain to get past bosses, not even hard.

This is also ignoring that there is one way that all bosses die, you never need to use a different attack or anything, the just make a goofy face with an effect ontop. There isnt a reward or pay off, you just gotta do it again cuz ♥♥♥♥ you.

3. The guns are boring
If you min-max, you just use spreader and roundabout, and assuming you dont the guns are still boring. The guns might as well just be bloat.

4. Immersion
Some of the attacks in almost all levels feel too video game-y for me to be immersed, thats also ignoring some bs hitboxes.

5. difficulty
This game isnt hard because of well made choices, its hard for the sake of being hard. Ive never one had an "ah ha" moment or anything like that. Games like Pizza Tower, Antonblast, and 7 Days To Die all have strats to defeat bosses and get thru levels, but Cuphead is so dead set on making a mini bullet hell to make a hard game.

6. movement
Its ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ boring.
I have played this game not only on steam, but on like 2 other platforms and I somehow still find it fun to play. This game is the perfect game if you like challenges.
its so peak it brings back core childhood memories, and the second ones coming out soon i decided to play it again for the memories, lets hope cuphead 2 is as legendary as the first

I have been waiting for the day this game would go on sale for a long time, and just two days ago, that day finally came. I completed this game in just one day (on normal mode), and I swear, this is the best indie game I have ever played. The handcrafted artwork, the levels, and the bosses are all very cleverly and meticulously designed. Currently, I am progressing to the DLC.

This game is a MUST BUY, totally worth it, both as a tribute to old-school animation and for delivering extremely engaging hardcore gameplay.

Very fun boss rush

Also nowhere near as hard as people made it out to be. Only nitpicks I had was that the run and guns weren't that fun, the smoke dash was so much better than any other power up for the most part, and the final boss was underwhelming (not a bad boss but didn't have a final boss feel)

The movement and controls felt very fluid and fun, the artsyle is just fantastic and makes you really appreciate the effort the devs put into this game, and the guns all felt unique and useful depending on the situation or personal playstyle. Scattershot was a personal favorite of mine.
 
Standout bosses in terms that gave me over 10+ attempts and felt like a real challenge relative to the rest of the others were the Robot, Bee, Dragon, Phantom Express, and the Final Boss (ik I said he was underwhelming but thats because the first phase took me 10+ attempts while it took another 3 or 4 to get past the next 3 phases. Good boss just not good as a final boss)

looking forward to playing the DLC since I just bought it

I got acquainted with Cuphead at a relatively young age, and when it came out on the PS4 I bought it immediately. All that training was useful here, so it wasn't like torture for me. This game is a very well known platformer with great visuals, and story. Some malding might come with it though.

Beautiful art, amazing music, challenging but fair gameplay. Getting 100% was painful, rewarding, and absolutely worth it.

10/10 — my controller survived, barely.
A challenging, yet fun indie game! Each frame is hand-drawn perfectly, enhancing the experience. This game was worth every cent, and it's best played with friends (If you don't like going solo)

Cuphead is a beautifully crafted run-and-gun game that combines stunning 1930s cartoon-inspired visuals with some of the most challenging boss battles in modern gaming.

Pros:

+ Outstanding hand-drawn art style inspired by classic cartoons.
+ Excellent soundtrack that perfectly matches the game's atmosphere.
+ Creative and memorable boss fights with unique mechanics.
+ Tight and responsive controls.
+ Extremely satisfying sense of accomplishment after defeating difficult bosses.
+ Co-op mode adds even more fun and chaos.

Cons:

- Very high difficulty that may discourage some players.
- Frequent deaths and repeated attempts are part of the experience.
- Limited margin for error during boss encounters.
- Some late-game bosses can feel overwhelming.

Cuphead is a fantastic game that I highly recommend, especially for players who enjoy challenging gameplay and boss-focused experiences. However, be prepared—this is a very hard game, and patience is essential. If you stick with it, the sense of achievement is well worth the effort.

A difficult but fair boss rush platformer game that makes you want to try and try again until you beat a boss. Every death you encounter feels like your own mistake, never the game being unfair. There are weapons and charms to alter gameplay in a way that you can fit Cuphead's combat to your own tastes

Music, environment and sound design are spectacular. Every second spent in game feels like you're transformed into olden black and white cartoons.

I still haven't beaten the DLC bosses yet, the base game alone is worth getting to dip your feet into this beautiful world.

Play with a friend or play solo, it's both an amazing time!

Okay.

After nearly 30 hours—and having earned the Platinum trophy—plus another 300 hours on the Nintendo Switch (where I also got the Platinum), I can confidently say that this is one of the best games I have ever played.

In other games, there are times when you turn down the volume to focus and put on your own music; however, this is one of those games where, instead of turning it down, you turn it *up*.

The game boasts some of the best visuals I have ever seen—a style that makes you feel as though you’ve stepped right into an old-school cartoon.

The gameplay is incredible; every single boss is completely unique, brimming with the creativity and passion of the Moldenhauer brothers.

Although the difficulty might initially feel like hitting a massive brick wall, you eventually get used to it after taking down the first three bosses.

If you ever get bored with the game, you can replay it multiple times with various self-imposed challenges—such as a "no-dash" run, for example, or restarting the run every time you take damage.

In short: 10/10.

[h1]I highly recommend the game, but be ready for a WALLOP![/h1]
[h2]I highly recommend Cuphead, as being an unique gameplay first game. The runs don't last long, and you can be done with it as quick as your skill allows it. You can finish bosses and levels in under 1 minute, or constant failing at it will make you stay hours and hours on the same boss. But you can always take a break after attempt. There is no grinding or wasting time, and most of the things I saw seem super fair. Altough the Clown and Cala Maria seems to have a bit of RNG as well.[/h2]

Finally got around to finish the game after giving a go in 2019 in co-op.
Tried it again this year in CO-OP but was brutal.
Tried it single player and managed to reach further.
The game slaps and slaps you super hard as well.
You have to mix and match abilities, and weapons for a particular scenario, and is cool that it allows you to experiment and create strategies.

The game looks cute, unique, and retro, but it plays like a charm. Parry is difficult at start. Dodging does not give you iframes, you have to use smoke grenade (ability) for that.
It looks easier in CO-OP but it doesn't, it balances 2 players with halving the damage made by players, but if one of you dies, you can revive him by parying his soul, or if he is dead dead, the damage made by the remaining player goes back to 100.

The thing is that in CO-OP having to do more damage, makes the phases longer, and so does the danger, coupled with more things on screen when playing with 2 players, it makes the CO-OP a Struggle. So it might make or break friendships.

*Oh and the RUN and GUN Levels in CO-OP are way harder, cause you have to stay on par with your friend, neither of you can go too much forward or stay back. It makes it such a much challenge, trying to avoid the enemies, and matching your friend movements.*


The DAMN Clown, and the third island was super hard and way about my weight class, but after multiple times of almost finishing a level I did it.
In the end... the King Dice and The Devil are so much harder than everything in the game, is an absolute exercise in skill and control, but after almost breaking my controller or giving up I did it.

The game is hard, but it's so great seeing how from 1% progress you crawl closer and closer to finishing a boss. A thought that felt impossible some minutes... hours ago looks achievabale.

I have to say that in the end from the joy of beating a boss, it transformed in to relief for finishing the boss and getting closer to the end game.

[h3]AND Then... you found out there is a DLC - With a new character, with new gameplay mechanics, with even harder bosses with more mechanics.[/h3]

I thought that playing the game recently will make it easier, no... the new character at begining seriously threw you off as you have to learn new movement mechanics. The difficulty was way higher, and I seriously struggled to beat the 2-3 bosses onto it... That damn Cow is such a sweat fest. The plane level with the shifting perspective, hard but great, unique.
But later and later, I got closer, and the hardest ending bosses I did them quite fast, the snowcone doing it like in the 1st attempt of the next day. 

There is also a Gauntlet that challenges you mechanically, mostly your parries, movement and patter recornition, that is just a mechanical challenge and while I was mad that I sucked at it, that was the part that I liked the most, knewing I should be able to do it, if you just give it a bit of time and attention. You had to decide when to commit and when not to commit to doing damage.

Managed to finish the DLC story but there is still that Broken Relic. I heard that there is a trick to summon the Djimmi in Cuphead for more health, but honestly I am not sure if I will go for that challenge. You have to beat some of the base game bosses and the DLC Boss to get some points to unlock the rune's potential.

Managed to get all the coins and bought myself all the weapons and charms.
Intresting game I thought it was cute and smily then it punches you in the soul. (holy aura)
its extremely difficult but the artstyle and the game overall is amazing.
It is a really good game if you want to challenge yourself and victory feels insanely good especially after you failed so much.

This game is still so ahead of its time. The art direction is insane, the gameplay is great, and it has difficulty.
Please play this if you haven't already!

9/10
